title: Canada World Cup 2026 Roster: Marsch's Squad, Alphonso Davies Status
original_text:
Canada's World Cup 2026 squad: Marsch's 26 and how far they can go
Jesse Marsch named Canada's 26-man World Cup 2026 squad on May 29, 2026, the final roster for the country's first home World Cup. Alphonso Davies made the list and captains the side, but a hamstring injury leaves him doubtful for the June 12 opener against Bosnia and Herzegovina. Below are all 26 players by position, Canada's Group B fixtures, the Davies update, and where to back the team among our World Cup 2026 betting sites.
Last updated: June 1, 2026. This is Canada's final 26-player squad as submitted to FIFA. Tournament rules allow an injured player to be replaced up to 24 hours before the opening match, so the list reflects the roster as confirmed on announcement night.
(Photo by Michael Owens/Getty Images)
Canada World Cup 2026 roster TL;DR
Head coach: Jesse Marsch
Roster status: Final 26-man squad, announced May 29, 2026
Captain and key player: Alphonso Davies (Bayern Munich), doubtful for the opener
Group: Group B, with Bosnia and Herzegovina, Qatar and Switzerland
First match: June 12 vs Bosnia and Herzegovina, BMO Field, Toronto (3:00 PM ET)
Squad makeup: 3 goalkeepers, 9 defenders, 10 midfielders, 4 forwards
All three group games are at home, in Toronto and Vancouver, before any knockout matches.
Canada World Cup 2026 roster: full squad list
(Photo by Stephen Maturen/Getty Images)
Twenty-six players, drawn from clubs across Europe, MLS and the Croatian and Belgian top flights, make up the squad Marsch takes into the home tournament. The list groups every player by position, with the club each was attached to at selection. Alphonso Davies is the one name carrying a fitness question into the opener; the rest of the squad is available.
PositionPlayerClubNote
Goalkeeper Maxime Crépeau Orlando City SC
Goalkeeper Owen Goodman Barnsley FC On loan from Crystal Palace FC
Goalkeeper Dayne St. Clair Inter Miami FC
Defender Moïse Bombito OGC Nice

Defender Joel Waterman Chicago Fire FC
Midfielder Ali Ahmed Norwich City FC
Midfielder Tajon Buchanan Villarreal CF
Midfielder Mathieu Choinière LAFC
Midfielder Stephen Eustáquio FC Porto
Midfielder Marcelo Flores Tigres UANL
Midfielder Ismaël Koné U.S. Sassuolo Calcio
Midfielder Liam Millar Hull City FC
Midfielder Jonathan Osorio Toronto FC
Midfielder Nathan Saliba R.S.C. Anderlecht
Midfielder Jacob Shaffelburg LAFC
Forward Jonathan David Juventus FC
Forward Promise David Royale Union Saint-Gilloise
Forward Cyle Larin RCD Mallorca
Forward Tani Oluwaseyi Villarreal CF
Squad total: 26 (3 goalkeepers, 9 defenders, 10 midfielders, 4 forwards).
Is Alphonso Davies in Canada's World Cup 2026 squad?
Yes. Alphonso Davies is in the squad and will captain Canada, though he is doubtful for the June 12 opener against Bosnia and Herzegovina as he recovers from a hamstring injury sustained in May. Marsch has said he expects Davies to play a part in the tournament, even if the opener arrives too soon.
The Bayern Munich left-back is the most recognizable name on the roster and Canada's most dangerous outlet running from the back. Canada Soccer kept him in the final 26 rather than leave him out over the opener, and the staff have treated sitting him for the first match as a precaution, not a setback. The aim is to have him available for the later group games in Vancouver and any knockout run.
Until he is back in full training, Canada has full-back cover in Richie Laryea and Alistair Johnston, and Marsch is likely to reshape the left side for the opener. Davies' fitness is the single biggest variable in how far the co-hosts can go, so this section will be updated as his status changes.
Who was left out of Canada's World Cup 2026 roster?
Marsch cut six players from the 32-man training camp in Charlotte, North Carolina to reach the final 26. The players who missed out were:
Zorhan Bassong (defender, Sporting Kansas City)
Jamie Knight-Lebel (defender, Bristol City, on loan at Swindon Town)
Ralph Priso (defender, Vancouver Whitecaps)
Jayden Nelson (midfielder, Austin FC)
Daniel Jebbison (forward, Preston North End, on loan from Bournemouth)
Jacen Russell-Rowe (forward, Toulouse)
Kamal Miller, a starter at the 2022 World Cup, was left out of the training camp earlier in the week and so did not reach the final-cut stage. Most of the closing decisions came down to fitness and squad balance rather than current form.
Canada World Cup 2026 fixtures
BMO Field (Photo by Michael Chisholm/Getty Images)
All three of Canada's Group B games are at home. The co-hosts open in Toronto, then move west to Vancouver for the final two matches. If Canada advances, its round of 32 and round of 16 games would also be played at BC Place.
DateOpponentVenueTime (ET)
Fri, June 12 Bosnia and Herzegovina BMO Field, Toronto 3:00 PM
Thu, June 18 Qatar BC Place, Vancouver 6:00 PM
Wed, June 24 Switzerland BC Place, Vancouver 3:00 PM
All times Eastern. For the latest match prices as the group unfolds, our soccer odds page tracks Canada's best betting sites. If Canada does well we could see matches against Argentina or Brazil on the bracket.

The best available price in each column is in bold. For the full list of books covering the tournament, see our World Cup 2026 betting sites guide.

“Odds were captured simultaneously on June 1, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET and move constantly. Confirm the live price at the sportsbook before placing any bet. 19+ (18+ in Alberta, Manitoba and Quebec). Please play responsibly. ”
Sol Fayerman-HansenEditor-in-Chief
Editor's take: Canada's World Cup 2026 chances
“A home World Cup changes the maths for Canada. Three group games in front of their own crowds, in a group where only Switzerland really worries me, gives this side a clear path to the knockout rounds for the first time. Reaching the round of 32 would already be the best result in the country's World Cup history. The swing factor is obvious. If Davies is close to full fitness by the Vancouver games, Canada has the attacking quality to win a group match and push Switzerland for top spot. If the hamstring lingers, Marsch leans harder on Jonathan David's goals and a compact back line, and the margins get thinner. Either way, getting out of the group is the realistic target rather than a deep run, and for a Canadian side on home soil, that is still a result worth backing. ”
Sol Fayerman-HansenEditor-in-Chief
Roster status and changes
This is Canada's final 26-man squad, submitted ahead of the June 1 FIFA deadline. Under tournament rules, a player ruled out by a serious injury can be replaced up to 24 hours before Canada's first match, so a late change is still possible if a fitness issue worsens.
Fitness is the main storyline. Alongside Davies, centre-back Moïse Bombito has come back from a leg injury and said he is ready for the opener. Forward Promise David returned from hip surgery earlier in the year, and defender Alfie Jones has worked back from an ankle problem. In goal, Maxime Crépeau and Dayne St. Clair are competing for the starting job, with the pre-tournament friendlies against Uzbekistan and the Republic of Ireland used to settle the order.

FAQs on Team Canada at World Cup 2026
Common Questions on Team Canada Soccer
Who is in Canada's World Cup 2026 squad?
Jesse Marsch named 26 players: three goalkeepers, nine defenders, 10 midfielders and four forwards. The headline names are captain Alphonso Davies (Bayern Munich), forward Jonathan David (Juventus) and midfielder Tajon Buchanan (Villarreal). The squad mixes Europe-based players with several from MLS, and 13 of the 26 return from Canada's 2022 World Cup roster. The full list by position is in the table above.
Is Alphonso Davies playing at the World Cup?
Davies is in the squad and is Canada's captain, but he is doubtful for the June 12 opener against Bosnia and Herzegovina while he recovers from a hamstring injury. Marsch expects him to feature at some stage, with the later group games in Vancouver a more realistic target than the opener. His status will be updated here as Canada confirms his fitness.
Who is Canada's head coach at World Cup 2026?
Jesse Marsch is Canada's head coach. He was appointed in 2024 and signed a contract extension ahead of the tournament, and this is his first World Cup in charge. Marsch named the final 26-man squad on May 29, 2026 in a live television special, with each player revealed on the EdgeWalk at the CN Tower in Toronto.
When did Canada announce its World Cup 2026 roster?
Canada Soccer announced the final 26-man squad on Friday, May 29, 2026, on TSN, CTV, Crave and RDS. The list was trimmed from a 32-player training camp in Charlotte, North Carolina. June 1 was the FIFA deadline for final squads, so Canada's roster is official, subject only to the standard injury-replacement window before the first match.
What group is Canada in at World Cup 2026?
Canada is in Group B with Bosnia and Herzegovina, Qatar and Switzerland. All three group games are at home: Toronto for the opener, then Vancouver for the next two. The top two teams in each group advance to the round of 32, along with the eight best third-placed teams across the 48-nation tournament.
When is Canada's first World Cup 2026 match?
Canada opens against Bosnia and Herzegovina on Friday, June 12, 2026 at BMO Field in Toronto, kickoff 3:00 PM ET. The co-hosts then face Qatar on June 18 and Switzerland on June 24, both at BC Place in Vancouver. Canada is still seeking its first win at a men's World Cup after appearances in 1986 and 2022.
